nodes.name: Submit invoice + supporting documents
nodes.documentation: Supplier submits the invoice via the authenticated external form (email + OTP), scoped to own records. Fields: invoiceNumber (text, mandatory), invoiceDate (date, mandatory), poReference (ref PO, mandatory), currency (dropdown of configured currencies, default = PO currency), lines [{poLineId (ref), description (text), quantity (number>0), unitPrice (number>=0), taxCodeId (ref TaxCode), taxableAmount (number), taxAmount (number)}], invoiceTotalInclTax (number>0, mandatory). Documents (AB package): invoice (mandatory), packingList, certificateOfOrigin, awbBl, bayan, healthCert, dgDeclaration (per import). Owning role: Supplier (invoice.capture submit). The buyer may assemble the package on the supplier's behalf (invoice.capture conditional). [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 7.5 records | source: AB invoice package + data-model Invoice]

nodes.name: Capture invoice in AP, normalize to base currency
nodes.documentation: Finance-Maker captures/validates the invoice into accounts payable. The FX service normalizes invoiceTotalInclTax to base currency (convertToBase: base/null -> unchanged; else live rate rounded 2dp; on failure return original unconverted, never throws). Tax is a first-class line attribute so the match reconciles amounts INCLUDING tax; reverse-charge / import VAT flagged for imports; input-VAT/GST credit tracked on recoverable tax. Owning role: Finance-Maker (invoice.capture). Captures realized-FX basis (PO date rate vs payment date rate computed later). [SCOR S2.7 | source: build-new + platform-services FX/tax]

nodes.name: Confirm service milestone acceptance
nodes.documentation: Two-way path only. For service/contract POs, the PaymentMilestone acceptance replaces the GRN as the third leg's proxy: confirm milestoneId acceptanceStatus == accepted before clearing, so payment releases on acceptance. Fields: milestoneId (ref), acceptanceStatus (dropdown {pending|accepted|rejected}), paymentReleaseOnAcceptance (boolean). Owning role: Engineering / requester acceptance. If no milestone (simple service), proceeds straight to two-way match. [SCOR S2.7 | source: data-model PaymentMilestone + AB engineering completion]

nodes.name: Buyer resolves price variance
nodes.documentation: Buyer reviews a price-variance exception (invoice unit price differs from PO agreed price beyond tolerance). Resolution (dropdown, mandatory): accept (price valid, e.g. agreed change) | adjust (correct PO/invoice price within editable window) | credit-note (supplier reduces payable) | debit-note (buyer raises against supplier) | reject (send back to supplier). Owning role: Buyer (match.resolveException price; creditNote/debitNote.manage conditional). [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model resolution set]

nodes.name: Receiving resolves quantity / missing-GR
nodes.documentation: Receiving reviews qty-over (invoice qty > GRN accepted), qty-under (invoice qty < GRN), or missing-GR (no GRN found for a goods invoice). Resolution (dropdown, mandatory): accept (e.g. raise/correct a GRN, then re-match) | adjust (correct received qty) | credit-note (over-billed qty) | debit-note | reject. missing-GR is resolved by raising the GRN (diagram 08) or confirming the line is two-way (service). Owning role: Receiving (match.resolveException qty). [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model qty routing]

nodes.name: Tax/Compliance resolves tax mismatch
nodes.documentation: Tax/Compliance reviews a tax-mismatch (invoice tax code / rate / amount does not reconcile with the PO line tax or jurisdiction rule, including reverse-charge on imports). Resolution (dropdown, mandatory): accept (tax correct) | adjust (correct taxCodeId / taxAmount) | credit-note | debit-note | reject. Confirms recoverable input-VAT/GST credit eligibility. Owning role: Tax/Compliance (match.resolveException tax). [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model tax routing + tax service]

nodes.name: Hold / reject duplicate invoice
nodes.documentation: Finance-Maker holds a DUPLICATE-INVOICE: no payable is created. Resolution (dropdown): reject (confirmed duplicate -> closed, supplier notified) | accept (false positive: distinct invoice with coincidental key -> override with note, re-route to match). No payment can be scheduled while a duplicate hold is open. Owning role: Finance-Maker. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model DUPLICATE-INVOICE control]

nodes.name: Approve invoice before accounts (SoD)
nodes.documentation: Invoice approval before it flows to accounts/payment. The matched (or note-adjusted) invoice/package is approved by the configured approver (AB: Procurement Head / PO-head approves the invoice package). SoD: the approver must NOT be the receiver of the same goods (receiver != invoice approver); buyer approval is conditional and not allowed if the buyer was the receiver. Owning role: Approver (invoice.approve; conditional PO-head/buyer). Sets Invoice.approvedBy. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.4 | source: AB invoice-package approval + role-matrix SoD]

nodes.name: Two-way match (PO + invoice, incl. tax)
nodes.documentation: Match the invoice against the PO only (no GRN). Checks within configurable MatchTolerance: pricePercent = abs(invoiceUnitPrice - poAgreedPrice)/poAgreedPrice; qtyPercent = abs(invoiceQty - poQty)/poQty; absolute amount band on invoiceTotalInclTax vs PO value incl. tax. Within all bands -> matched; outside any -> exception. Defaults configurable per tenant. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model MatchTolerance]

nodes.name: Three-way match (PO + GRN + invoice, incl. tax)
nodes.documentation: Match the invoice against PO AND GRN. Checks within configurable MatchTolerance: price variance pricePercent (invoice vs PO agreed price), quantity qtyPercent (invoice qty vs GRN accepted qty, NOT ordered qty), absolute amount band on invoiceTotalInclTax vs (GRN accepted qty x agreed price + tax). Within all bands -> matched; outside any -> exception. Tax amounts must reconcile (amounts INCLUDING tax). [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model MatchResult THREE_WAY]

nodes.name: Auto-clear, relieve GR/IR
nodes.documentation: On matched: set MatchResult.matchStatus = matched. For three-way, clear the GrIrEntry: GrIrEntry.invoiceId set, clearedAmount = matched amount, status -> cleared (or partially-cleared for partial invoice), relieving the goods-received-not-invoiced liability. Posts the payable to the creditor ledger. For two-way there is no GR/IR to relieve; the payable posts directly. Emits audit + SSE. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: build-new GR/IR clearing]

nodes.name: Post credit / debit note to creditor ledger
nodes.documentation: Posts a CreditNote (supplier-issued, reduces payable) or DebitNote (buyer-raised) against the creditor ledger: fields linkedInvoiceId, linkedMatchExceptionId, amount, taxAmount, reason. The adjusted net payable then clears the GR/IR (three-way) or posts directly (two-way) and proceeds to approval. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: build-new credit/debit note]

nodes.name: Release approved payable to payment (diagram 10)
nodes.documentation: Approved, matched payable is released to the payment schedule (diagram 10). The creditor ledger reflects the net amount (after any credit/debit note and withholding). GR/IR is cleared for three-way lines. Emits audit + SSE. [SCOR S2.7 | source: build-new -> diagram 10]

nodes.name: Return invoice to supplier (rejected)
nodes.documentation: Rejected invoice is returned to the supplier with the reason (notification on the order thread); no payable, no payment. Supplier may resubmit a corrected invoice, which re-enters at submit. Terminal for the current invoice. [SCOR S2.7 | source: data-model resolution reject]

nodes.name: Duplicate invoice?
nodes.documentation: Standard AP duplicate control. Branch expression: EXISTS another invoice with same (supplierId AND invoiceNumber AND amount) -> DUPLICATE-INVOICE exception; else -> proceed to match-type gateway. Detection key = supplier + invoice number + amount. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model MatchException DUPLICATE-INVOICE]

nodes.name: GRN exists for this PO/category?
nodes.documentation: Match-type selector. Branch expression: a GRN exists for the PO/category (goods-receipt materials, S2 direct) -> THREE_WAY (PO + GRN + Invoice); no GRN exists (services / freight / spares received by Engineering, S3/T2/T3) -> TWO_WAY (PO + Invoice) plus milestone acceptance for service POs. Rule: three-way iff a GRN exists for the category, else two-way. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model match logic two-way vs three-way]

nodes.name: Within tolerance?
nodes.documentation: Branch expression: pricePercent <= tolerance.pricePercent AND qtyPercent <= tolerance.qtyPercent AND abs(amountVariance) <= tolerance.absolute (all incl. tax) -> matchStatus = matched (auto-clear); else -> matchStatus = exception with an exceptionReason. First-time-match rate and three-way-match exception rate are tracked KPIs. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model Match state machine]

nodes.name: Exception type -> route to resolver
nodes.documentation: Routes by MatchException.type. Branch expression: type == 'price-variance' -> Buyer (price exceptions); type in {'qty-over','qty-under','missing-GR'} -> Receiving (qty exceptions); type == 'tax-mismatch' -> Tax/Compliance (tax exceptions); type == 'DUPLICATE-INVOICE' -> Finance-Maker (hold/reject, no payable created). routedTo per data-model: buyer for price, receiving for qty, finance for tax. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model MatchException routing]

nodes.name: Resolution outcome?
nodes.documentation: Branch expression on resolution: resolution in {accept, adjust} -> re-run match (the corrected invoice/PO/GRN/tax re-enters matching); resolution in {credit-note, debit-note} -> post the note to the creditor ledger then clear the adjusted payable; resolution == reject -> return invoice to supplier (terminal for this invoice). credit-note reduces payable (supplier-issued, linkedInvoiceId/linkedMatchExceptionId); debit-note is buyer-raised. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model resolution + credit/debit note]

nodes.name: Goods received / service accepted
nodes.documentation: Entry trigger: a GRN was raised (materials, diagram 08) or a service/spares requisition was closed 'received as per order' (no GRN) or a service milestone was accepted. The supplier submits the invoice against the PO. [SCOR S2.7 Authorize Supplier Payment | source: build-new + AB invoice package]

nodes.name: Matched and approved -> payment (diagram 10)
nodes.documentation: Outcome: the invoice is matched (auto-cleared or resolved), approved, GR/IR relieved where applicable, payable posted to the creditor ledger, and handed to diagram 10 payments and installments. Rejected/duplicate invoices exit without a payable. [SCOR S2.7 | source: build-new]
